APPENDIX 2 
Example of multiple regions of interest 
A. LAB configuration; for active range dimensions, refer to Figure 1A. PFDM units shown on tripod at 1.5m above ground level for 
illustration purposes only; units can be positioned at any height to optimize vantage point onto experimental stage. 
i. Single full-scale region of interest, shown for reference 
ii. Two sectional regions of interest; user specifies region(s) of interest at the time of experiment initiation, or defaults to 
single

iii. “Fan” – multiple units arranged to maximize contiguous active volume. Note that adjacent active regions will need to 
be configured with adequate overlap to ensure target continuity. 
iv. “Cross-view” – two units arranged to maximize overlapping active volume to get multiple perspectives on the same 
scene. The two distinct coordinate systems for each unit would be harmonized into a single world coordinate system 
through mutual calibration. 
Client1 CONFIDENTIAL Page 6 of 9

Items.field:
General product information and other remarks:
Description of unit:
Items.value:
General product information and other remarks:
Description of unit:
Product1 is an electronic device designed for tracking small airborne targets, such as insects, using onboard IR illumination and imaging sensors. It operates on 48 VDC, with a power rating of 216 W, and supports continuous operation for up to one week. The unit can track over 1,000 non-intersecting and 100 intersecting targets within a configurable detection volume. The enclosure is robust, portable, and suitable for laboratory or field environments. Data is stored onboard and uploaded for post-processing when network access is available. Manufacturer: Manufacturer1, Model: 001.

Items.field: Description of model differences:
Items.value:
Description of model differences:
Product1 is available in two configurations: LAB (largest) and MINI (smallest). LAB configuration features a 3m x 3m x 10m active volume, blind to targets under 5m, while MINI configuration has a 3m x 3m x 3m active volume, blind to targets under 1m. Both share core functionality, but differ in detection range and physical size as specified by customer at production. No other model differences are indicated in the provided documentation or images.

Items.field:
Description of special features:
(HV circuits, high pressure systems etc.)
Items.value:
Description of special features:
The product operates at 48 VDC and 216 W, powered by an external AC/DC adapter. It features onboard IR illumination for day/night operation and advanced target tracking capabilities. Internally, it contains multiple camera modules and electronic boards, but there is no evidence of high voltage circuits, high pressure systems, or hazardous materials. The enclosure appears robust and sealed, likely for environmental protection, but no explicit special safety features such as HV or pressure systems are present based on available documentation and images.

• 
• 
• 
• 
Use this product only as specified. 
Only qualified personnel should perform service. 
Use only the power cord specified for this product and certified for the country of use. 
The PFMD is grounded through the grounding conductor in the power cord. To avoid electric 
shock, the grounding conductor must be connected to earth ground. 
Do not operate the PFMD with any c

• 300W power budget; capable of being solar-powered (solar panel, battery, etc. not 
included) 
• Integrated battery, allowing device to handle brownouts up to 10 minutes in duratio

Consumption 60W, maximum 
Ambient 
Temperature 
• Operational: 5°C - 45°C 
• Storage: -20°C - 60°C 
Ambient Humidity 0 – 90% RH 
 
Sun Exposure 
 
Direct sunlight may raise the operating temperature of the optoelectronic hardware beyond 
acceptable limits and may also impose excessive optical noise. Installations which would 
normally expose the PFMD to direct sunlight will need to erect a sunshade. 
Moisture 
Resistance* Indoor/outdoor, including capability to sustain high humidity and rain 
Du

normally expose the PFMD to direct sunlight will need to erect a sunshade. 
Moisture 
Resistance* 
Indoor/outdoor, including capability to sustain (but not necessarily operate in) high humidity and 
rain 
Dust Protected* Protected from limited dust ingress 
Electrical Safety Package to include UL/FCC certified power supply 
Emissions Compliant with FCC Class A (non-residential) regulations* 
Export Control N/A, but will need analysis to show compliance 
Labeling TBD 
Target User • Entomologists

6. Transport, Storage and Mounting 
a. The PFMD is delivered in packaging which was designed to keep it safe during transport and 
storage. Please retain this original packaging and use it to transport and store the PFMD when it 
is not being utilized for data collection. 
When carrying the PFMD outside of its packaging, please support it using both handles on the lid 
of the device. The handles can be folded flat against the lid when not in use.

6. Transport, Storage and Mounting 
a. The PFMD is delivered in packaging which was designed to keep it safe during transport and 
storage. Please retain this original packaging and use it to transport and store the PFMD when it 
is not being utilized for data collection. 
When carrying the PFMD outside of its packaging, please support it using both handles on the lid 
of the device. The handles can be folded flat against the lid when not in use.

Appendix A: Technical Specifications 
1. Product Description / Key Functionality 
Page | 11 
Illumination Onboard IR illumination to allow day or night operation 
Run Time 1-week continuous operation, including adequate onboard data storage (upload to web app for 
post-processing when network is available) 
 
 
 
Active Volume 
 
NOTE: for pictorial descriptions, please see Figure A10 
• 3m x 3m x RANGE square pyramid frustum, where RANGE is set at the time of unit 
production as specified by th

Resolution • Reliably tracks targets with characteristic length of 4mm or greater (i.e., mosquitoes) 
• Smaller targets are likely to be detected, but lower bound is TBD 
 
 
Recorded Data 
 
• 3-D position of each unique target, maintaining track continuity across specified active 
region(s), see APPENDIX 2

4. Recording Session 
a. Once the PFMD is powered on, the user interface (formally, the PFMD Capture Client) is 
accessible. The first step is to verify appropriate pointing of the PFMD. The default view of the 
PFMD user interface (Figure 4) depicts the area visible to each of the two cameras (left and 
right, in concert comprising the stereoscopic vision system). Use this view to aim the PFMD to 
optimize the experimental scene visible to the device.

FLOOR 
 
The PFMD may be placed on the floor if that is the optimal vantage point. 
The viewing angle can be adjusted using the tilting mechanism on the front 
face of the device; use the clamping lever to release and secure the tilting 
mechanism (Figure 3). 
CUSTOM Alternatively, the PFMD may be placed on any sturdy surface large enough to 
support the four rubberized feet on the base of the device.

text_support.text:
1. General Safety Precautions 
Please follow these safety precautions to avoid injury and prevent damage to the PFMD. 
• 
• 
• 
• 
Use this product only as specified. 
Only qualified personnel should perform service. 
Use only the power cord specified for this product and certified for the country of use. 
The PFMD is grounded through the grounding conductor in the power cord. To avoid electric 
shock, the grounding conductor must be connected to earth ground. 
Do not operate the PFMD with any c

2 STAKEHOLDERS 
3 REGULATORY 
Assume that no regulatory constraints exist 
* Note: as-built device will need to be tested to confirm compliance 
4 OPERATING ENVIRONMENT 
*IP54 Rated 
5 INTERFACES 
Client1 CONFIDENTIAL Page 3 of 9 
Hardware 
 
 
Electrical Power 
 
• 12V DC input; 110V - 220 V adapter included in kit 
• 300W power budget; capable of being solar-powered (solar panel, battery, etc. not 
included) 
• Integrated battery, allowing device to handle brownouts up to 10 minutes in duratio

normally expose the PFMD to direct sunlight will need to erect a sunshade. 
Moisture 
Resistance* 
Indoor/outdoor, including capability to sustain (but not necessarily operate in) high humidity and 
rain 
Dust Protected* Protected from limited dust ingress 
Electrical Safety Package to include UL/FCC certified power supply 
Emissions Compliant with FCC Class A (non-residential) regulations* 
Export Control N/A, but will need analysis to show compliance 
Labeling TBD 
Target User • Entomologists

2 STAKEHOLDERS 
3 REGULATORY 
Assume that no regulatory constraints exist 
* Note: as-built device will need to be tested to confirm compliance 
4 OPERATING ENVIRONMENT 
*IP54 Rated 
5 INTERFACES 
Client1 CONFIDENTIAL Page 3 of 9 
Hardware 
 
 
Electrical Power 
 
• 12V DC input; 110V - 220 V adapter included in kit 
• 300W power budget; capable of being solar-powered (solar panel, battery, etc. not 
included) 
• Integrated battery, allowing device to handle brownouts up to 10 minutes in duratio

Consumption 60W, maximum 
Ambient 
Temperature 
• Operational: 5°C - 45°C 
• Storage: -20°C - 60°C 
Ambient Humidity 0 – 90% RH 
 
Sun Exposure 
 
Direct sunlight may raise the operating temperature of the optoelectronic hardware beyond 
acceptable limits and may also impose excessive optical noise. Installations which would 
normally expose the PFMD to direct sunlight will need to erect a sunshade. 
Moisture 
Resistance* Indoor/outdoor, including capability to sustain high humidity and rain

Consumption 60W, maximum 
Ambient 
Temperature 
• Operational: 5°C - 45°C 
• Storage: -20°C - 60°C 
Ambient Humidity 0 – 90% RH 
 
Sun Exposure 
 
Direct sunlight may raise the operating temperature of the optoelectronic hardware beyond 
acceptable limits and may also impose excessive optical noise. Installations which would

Direct sunlight may raise the operating temperature of the optoelectronic hardware beyond 
acceptable limits and may also impose excessive optical noise. Installations which would 
normally expose the PFMD to direct sunlight will need to erect a sunshade. 
Moisture 
Resistance* Indoor/outdoor, including capability to sustain high humidity and rain

with a threaded tripod mount on the base.

The PFMD may be placed on the floor if that is the optimal vantage point. 
The viewing angle can be adjusted using the tilting mechanism on the front 
face of the device; use the clamping lever to release and secure the tilting 
mechanism (Figure 3).

iii. “Fan” – multiple units arranged to maximize contiguous active volume. Note that adjacent active regions will need to

iv. “Cross-view” – two units arranged to maximize overlapping active volume to get multiple perspectives on the same 
scene. The two distinct coordinate systems for each unit would be harmonized into a single world coordinate system 
through mutual calibration.

face of the device; use the clamping lever to release and secure the tilting

of the device. The handles can be folded flat against the lid when not in use.

text_support.text:
APPENDIX 2 
Example of multiple regions of interest 
A. LAB configuration; for active range dimensions, refer to Figure 1A. PFDM units shown on tripod at 1.5m above ground level for 
illustration purposes only; units can be positioned at any height to optimize vantage point onto experimental stage. 
i. Single full-scale region of interest, shown for reference 
ii. Two sectional regions of interest; user specifies region(s) of interest at the time of experiment initiation, or defaults to 
single

iii. “Fan” – multiple units arranged to maximize contiguous active volume. Note that adjacent active regions will need to 
be configured with adequate overlap to ensure target continuity. 
iv. “Cross-view” – two units arranged to maximize overlapping active volume to get multiple perspectives on the same 
scene. The two distinct coordinate systems for each unit would be harmonized into a single world coordinate system 
through mutual calibration. 
Client1 CONFIDENTIAL Page 6 of 9

accessible. The first step is to verify appropriate pointing of the PFMD. The default view of the 
PFMD user interface (Figure 4) depicts the area visible to each of the two cameras (left and 
right, in concert comprising the stereoscopic vision system). Use this view to aim the PFMD to 
optimize the experimental scene visible to the device.

c. A specified Region of Interest can be used to exclude areas of the scene which are not germane 
to the experiment. Setting the Region of Interest is accomplished using the ROI toggle and 
subsequently adjusting the green rectangular ROI box for each camera independently. 
Eliminating the Region of Interest is accomplished using the CLEAR ROI button. See Figure 6 for 
reference. 
Figure 6: Region of Interest selection 
d. Once the product is oriented appropriately and illumination and ROI are

4. Recording Session 
a. Once the PFMD is powered on, the user interface (formally, the PFMD Capture Client) is 
accessible. The first step is to verify appropriate pointing of the PFMD. The default view of the 
PFMD user interface (Figure 4) depicts the area visible to each of the two cameras (left and 
right, in concert comprising the stereoscopic vision system). Use this view to aim the PFMD to 
optimize the experimental scene visible to the device.
